---
name: Feature Request
about: Suggest a new feature or enhancement for OxiMedia
title: '[FEATURE] '
labels: ['enhancement']
assignees: ''
---

## Feature Description

A clear and concise description of the feature you'd like to see added.

## Motivation

Why is this feature needed? What problem does it solve?

## Proposed Solution

Describe how you envision this feature working. Include:

- API design (if applicable)
- User interface changes (if applicable)
- Example usage code

### Example Usage

```rust
// Show how you'd like to use this feature
```

## Alternatives Considered

Have you considered any alternative solutions or features? Why is your proposed solution better?

## Additional Context

Add any other context, screenshots, or examples about the feature request here.

## Implementation Notes

If you have ideas about how this feature could be implemented:

- Which crates would be affected?
- What dependencies might be needed?
- Are there any potential breaking changes?
- Performance considerations?

## Related Features

- List any related features or issues using #issue_number
- Link to any relevant discussions or RFCs

## Scope

Please indicate the scope of this feature:

- [ ] Core functionality (codec, container, I/O)
- [ ] Computer vision
- [ ] Audio processing
- [ ] Networking
- [ ] CLI tool
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Developer experience
- [ ] Other: ___________

## Willingness to Contribute

- [ ] I am willing to implement this feature myself
- [ ] I am willing to help review a PR for this feature
- [ ] I need someone else to implement this feature

## Patent/Legal Considerations

Does this feature involve any patented technologies or require careful legal consideration?

- [ ] No, this is patent-free
- [ ] Unsure, needs investigation
- [ ] Yes, requires patent review: (explain)

## Checklist

- [ ] I have searched existing issues and discussions
- [ ] This feature aligns with OxiMedia's patent-free philosophy
- [ ] I have provided a clear use case and motivation
- [ ] I have considered backwards compatibility
